questions.question: A right triangle has an angle of 35° and the side opposite to it measures 12 cm. Find the length of the hypotenuse. Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: Use sin(35°) = opposite/hypotenuse. So hypotenuse = 12/sin(35°) ≈ 12/0.574 ≈ 20.9 cm.

questions.question: On the unit circle, if cos(θ) = −3/5 and θ is in the second quadrant, find sin(θ).
questions.explanation: Use sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1. So sin²(θ) = 1 − (9/25) = 16/25. Since θ is in quadrant II, sin(θ) > 0, so sin(θ) = 4/5.

questions.question: A ladder leans against a wall at a 60° angle to the ground. If the ladder is 10 feet long, how high does it reach on the wall?
questions.explanation: The height is the side opposite the 60° angle. Use sin(60°) = height/10. So height = 10 · (√3/2) = 5√3 feet.

questions.question: Simplify: sin²(x) + sin²(x)·cot²(x)
questions.explanation: Factor: sin²(x)[1 + cot²(x)]. Since 1 + cot²(x) = csc²(x) = 1/sin²(x), we get sin²(x) · (1/sin²(x)) = 1.

questions.question: If tan(θ) = 7/24 and θ is in the first quadrant, find cos(θ).
questions.explanation: In a right triangle with opposite = 7 and adjacent = 24, the hypotenuse = √(49 + 576) = √625 = 25. So cos(θ) = adjacent/hypotenuse = 24/25.

questions.question: What is the value of sin(150°)?
questions.explanation: 150° = 180° − 30°. Using the supplementary angle identity: sin(150°) = sin(30°) = 1/2.
